title: Process operatives n.e.c.
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Lay and install carpet from rolls or blocks on floors. Install padding and trim flooring materials. Apply blocks, strips, or sheets of shock-absorbing, sound-deadening, or decorative coverings to floors. (Based on 55 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

work_context.element_name: Face-to-Face Discussions with Individuals and Within Teams
Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General
Coordinate or Lead Others in Accomplishing Work Activities
Health and Safety of Other Workers
Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions
In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment
In an Enclosed Vehicle or Operate Enclosed Equipment
Exposed to Sounds, Noise Levels that are Distracting or Uncomfortable
Exposed to Very Hot or Cold Temperatures
Exposed to Extremely Bright or Inadequate Lighting Conditions
Exposed to Cramped Work Space, Awkward Positions
Exposed to Minor Burns, Cuts, Bites, or Stings
Spend Time Climbing Ladders, Scaffolds, or Poles
Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance
Spend Time Using Your Hands to Handle, Control, or Feel Objects, Tools, or Controls
Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Depress pedals to collapse drums after processing is complete.

Pull plies from supply racks, and align plies with edges of drums. Buff tires according to specifications for width and undertread depth. Place tires into molds for new tread. Trim excess rubber and imperfections during retreading processes. Measure tires to determine mold size requirements. Roll hand rollers over rebuilt casings, exerting pressure to ensure adhesion between camelbacks and casings. Brush or spray solvents onto plies to ensure adhesion, alternating direction of each ply to strengthen tires. Rub cement sticks on drum edges to provide adhesive surfaces for plies. Fit inner tubes and final layers of rubber onto tires. Clean and paint completed tires. Fill cuts and holes in tires, using hot rubber. Wind chafers and breakers onto plies. Position ply stitcher rollers and drums according to width of stock, using hand tools and gauges. Align treads with guides, start drums to wind treads onto plies, and slice ends. Cut plies at splice points, and press ends together to form continuous bands. Inspect worn tires for faults, cracks, cuts, and nail holes, to determine if tires are suitable for retreading. Start rollers that bond tread and plies as drums revolve. Depress pedals to rotate drums, and wind specified numbers of plies around drums to form tire bodies. Build semi-raw rubber treads onto buffed tire casings to prepare tires for vulcanization in recapping or retreading processes.

Measure and mark guidelines on surfaces or foundations, using chalk lines and dividers. Apply adhesive cement to floor or wall material to join and adhere foundation material. Roll and press sheet wall and floor covering into cement base to smooth and finish surface, using hand roller. Cut covering and foundation materials according to blueprints and sketches. Cut flooring material to fit around obstructions. Form a smooth foundation by stapling plywood or Masonite over the floor or by brushing waterproof compound onto surface and filling cracks with plaster, putty, or grout to seal pores. Trim excess covering materials, tack edges, and join sections of covering material to form tight joints. Inspect surface to be covered to ensure that it is firm and dry. Lay out, position, and apply shock-absorbing, sound-deadening, or decorative coverings to floors, walls, and cabinets, following guidelines to keep courses straight and create designs. Determine traffic areas and decide location of seams. Inspect floors for smoothness. Remove excess cement to clean finished surfaces. Disconnect and remove appliances, light fixtures, and worn floor and wall covering from floors, walls, and cabinets. Heat and soften floor covering materials to patch cracks or fit floor coverings around irregular surfaces, using blowtorch. Clean up before and after installation, including vacuuming carpet and discarding remnant pieces. Remove excess glue from joints, using knives, scrapers, or wood chisels. Apply filler compound and coats of finish to floors to seal wood. Attach sandpaper to rollers of sanding machines.

Guide sanding machines over surfaces of floors until surfaces are smooth. Scrape and sand floor edges and areas inaccessible to floor sanders, using scrapers, disk-type sanders, and sandpaper. Cut and trim carpet to fit along wall edges, openings, and projections, finishing the edges with a wall trimmer. Sweep, scrape, sand, or chip dirt and irregularities to clean base surfaces, correcting imperfections that may show through the covering. Join edges of carpet and seam edges where necessary, by sewing or by using tape with glue and heated carpet iron. Cut and bind material. Finish and dress the joints and wipe excess grout from between tiles, using a damp sponge. Nail tack strips around area to be carpeted or use old strips to attach edges of new carpet. Fasten metal treads across door openings or where carpet meets flooring to hold carpet in place. Study blueprints and examine the surface to be covered to determine the amount of material needed. Prepare surfaces for tiling by attaching lath or waterproof paper, or by applying a cement mortar coat to a metal screen.

Measure and mark surfaces to be tiled, following blueprints. Mix, apply, and spread plaster, concrete, mortar, cement, mastic, glue or other adhesives to form a bed for the tiles, using brush, trowel, and screed. Apply mortar to tile back, position the tile, and press or tap with trowel handle to affix tile to base. Cut and shape tile to fit around obstacles and into odd spaces and corners, using hand and power cutting tools. Buff and vacuum floors to ensure their cleanliness prior to the application of finish. Determine and implement the best layout to achieve a desired pattern. Install and anchor fixtures in designated positions, using hand tools. Cut tile backing to required size, using shears. Remove any old tile, grout, and adhesive using chisels and scrapers and clean the surface carefully. Lay and set mosaic tiles to create decorative wall, mural, and floor designs. Remove and replace cracked or damaged tile. Measure and cut metal lath to size for walls and ceilings, using tin snips. Mix and apply mortar or cement to edges and ends of drain tiles to seal halves and joints. Apply a sealer to make grout stain- and water-resistant. Cut, surface, polish, and install marble and granite or install pre-cast terrazzo, granite, or marble units. Take measurements and study floor sketches to calculate the area to be carpeted and the amount of material needed. Move furniture from the area to be carpeted and remove old carpet and padding. Inspect the surface to be covered to determine its condition, and correct any imperfections that might show through carpet or cause carpet to wear unevenly. Roll out, measure, mark, and cut carpeting to size with a carpet knife, following floor sketches and allowing extra carpet for final fitting. Plan the layout of the carpet, allowing for expected traffic patterns and placing seams for best appearance and longest wear. Stretch carpet to align with walls and ensure a smooth surface, and press carpet in place over tack strips or use staples, tape, tacks, or glue to hold carpet in place. Cut carpet padding to size and install padding, following the prescribed method. Install carpet on some floors using adhesive, following the prescribed method.

Signal coworkers to synchronize flow of materials. Record production data, such as weight and amount of product processed, type of product, and time and temperature of processing. Weigh or measure products, using scale hoppers or scale conveyors. Observe flow of materials and listen for machine malfunctions, such as jamming or spillage, and notify supervisors if corrective actions fail. Clean installation sites, mixing and storage areas, tools, machines, and equipment, and store materials and equipment. Measure designated amounts of ingredients for terrazzo or grout, according to standard formulas and specifications, using graduated containers and scales, and load ingredients into portable mixer. Mold expansion joints and edges, using edging tools, jointers, or straightedges. Spread, level, or smooth concrete or terrazzo mixtures to form bases or finished surfaces, using rakes, shovels, hand or power trowels, hand or power screeds, or floats. Grind curved surfaces or areas inaccessible to surfacing machine, such as stairways or cabinet tops, with portable hand grinder. Grind surfaces with a power grinder, or polish surfaces with polishing or surfacing machines. Position and secure moisture membrane and wire mesh in preparation for pouring base materials for terrazzo installation. Modify mixing, grouting, grinding, or cleaning procedures according to the type of installation or material used. Wash polished terrazzo surfaces, using cleaner and water, and apply sealer and curing agent according to manufacturer's specifications, using brush or sprayer. Mix cement, sand, and water to produce concrete, grout, or slurry, using hoe, trowel, tamper, scraper, or concrete-mixing machine. Sprinkle colored marble or stone chips, powdered steel, or coloring powder over surfaces to produce prescribed finishes. Wet surfaces to prepare for bonding, fill holes and cracks with grout or slurry, and smooth with a trowel. Repair concrete by cutting out damaged areas, drilling holes for reinforcing rods, and positioning reinforcing rods, using a power saw and drill. Fill slight grinding depressions with matching grout material and hand-trowel for a smooth, uniform surface. Set temperature and time controls, light ovens, burners, driers, or roasters, and start equipment, such as conveyors, cylinders, blowers, driers, or pumps.

Clean and salt hides. Produce hamburger meat and meat trimmings. Process primal parts into cuts that are ready for retail use. Trim, clean, or cure animal hides. Wrap dressed carcasses or meat cuts. Grind meat into hamburger, and into trimmings used to prepare sausages, luncheon meats, and other meat products. Saw, split, or scribe carcasses into smaller portions to facilitate handling. Trim head meat, and sever or remove parts of animals' heads or skulls. Skin sections of animals or whole animals. Shackle hind legs of animals to raise them for slaughtering or skinning. Stun animals prior to slaughtering. Shave or singe and defeather carcasses, and wash them in preparation for further processing or packaging. Tend assembly lines, performing a few of the many cuts needed to process a carcass. Slit open, eviscerate, and trim carcasses of slaughtered animals. Cut, trim, skin, sort, and wash viscera of slaughtered animals to separate edible portions from offal. Remove bones, and cut meat into standard cuts in preparation for marketing. Sever jugular veins to drain blood and facilitate slaughtering. Clear or dislodge blockages in bins, screens, or other equipment, using poles, brushes, or mallets. Assist customers in the selection of tile and grout. Negotiate with representatives from supply companies to determine order details. Cure, smoke, tenderize, and preserve meat. Supervise other butchers or meat cutters. Record quantity of meat received and issued to cooks or keep records of meat sales. Prepare special cuts of meat ordered by customers. Estimate requirements and order or requisition meat supplies to maintain inventories. Shape, lace, and tie roasts, using a boning knife, skewer, and twine. Receive, inspect, and store meat upon delivery to ensure meat quality. Cut, trim, bone, tie, and grind meats, such as beef, pork, poultry, and fish, to prepare in cooking form. Prepare and place meat cuts and products in the display counter to appear attractive and catch the shopper's eye. Wrap, weigh, label, and price cuts of meat. Obtain and distribute specified meat or carcass. Separate meats and byproducts into specified containers and seal containers. Clean and paint completed tires. Finish and dress the joints and wipe excess grout from between tiles, using a damp sponge. Pull plies from supply racks, and align plies with edges of drums. Buff tires according to specifications for width and undertread depth. Place tires into molds for new tread.
Negotiate with representatives from supply companies to determine order details. Assist customers in the selection of tile and grout.

Chip, scrape, or grind high spots, ridges, or rough projections to finish concrete, using pneumatic chisels, power grinders, or hand tools; clean chipped area with a wire brush and observe surface to determine if it is rough or uneven; wet concrete surface and rub with stone to smooth surface and obtain specified finish.
Build wooden molds, clamp molds around areas to be repaired, and set them up to the proper depth and alignment.

Signal truck driver to position truck for pouring concrete, and move chute to direct concrete on forms; monitor environmental conditions affecting the curing of concrete throughout the process and ensure materials are properly spread and levelled.
Operate or tend equipment to roast, bake, dry, or cure food items.
Install, waterproof or restore concrete surfaces, using appropriate compounds; apply hardening and sealing compounds to cure and restore the surface of concrete.

Observe machine operations, control boards, and gauges to detect malfunctions; notify workers of defects and make necessary machine adjustments; record details of any equipment malfunctions and maintain extruding and forming machines, using hand tools.
Move materials, tools and work devices to work areas, manually or with a wheelbarrow; operate power vibrators to compact concrete and perform final touch-ups with sandpaper or steel wool.
Inspect products and machinery to verify conformance to specifications and determine if machine adjustments are needed.
Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, including woodworking, Weld, weave, or follow design specifications.
Perform administrative tasks related to manufacturing, such as computing costs, keeping records, and estimating material needs.

Mark cutting lines or identifying information on stock, using marking pencils, rulers, or scribes. Measure and mark dimensions of parts on paper or lumber stock prior to cutting, following blueprints, to ensure a tight fit and quality product. Mark identifying information such as colors or codes on patterns, parts, and templates to indicate assembly methods and details. Verify dimensions or check the quality or fit of pieces to ensure adherence to specifications. Inspect equipment or units to detect leaks or malfunctions, shutting equipment down if necessary. Observe and compare colors and consistencies of products to instrument readings and laboratory and standard test results. Examine samples to verify qualities such as clarity, cleanliness, consistency, dryness, or texture.

Change or replace saw blades, cables, cutter heads, and grinding wheels, using hand tools. Clean and lubricate cutting machines, conveyors, blades, saws, or knives, using steam hoses, scrapers, brushes, or oil cans. Make minor repairs, lubricate, and maintain equipment, using hand tools. Repair and maintain equipment, objects, or parts, using hand tools. Monitor operation of cutting or slicing machines to detect malfunctions or to determine whether supplies need replenishment. Adjust machine controls to alter position, alignment, speed, or pressure.

Feed stock into cutting machines, onto conveyors, or under cutting blades, by threading, guiding, pushing, or turning handwheels. Press buttons, pull levers, or depress pedals to start and operate cutting and slicing machines. Start machines to verify setups, and make any necessary adjustments. Turn valves or move controls to admit, drain, separate, filter, clarify, mix, or transfer materials. Start agitators, shakers, conveyors, pumps, or centrifuge machines. Load and adjust workpieces onto equipment or work tables, using hand tools. Align and position materials being joined to ensure accurate application of adhesive or heat sealing. Move controls to adjust, start, or stop equipment during grinding and polishing processes.

Stack and sort cut material for packaging, further processing, or shipping, according to types and sizes of material. Remove completed materials or products from cutting or slicing machines, and stack or store them for additional processing. Collect and store patterns and lumber. Remove defective or substandard materials from machines, and readjust machine components so that products meet standards.

Test production runs and make adjustments as necessary to ensure that completed products meet standards and specifications. Perform test production runs and make adjustments as necessary to ensure that completed products meet standards and specifications. Maintain production records such as quantities, dimensions, and thicknesses of materials processed.

Cement the edges of the paved area. Fill and smooth cracks or depressions, remove marks and imperfections, and repair broken parts, using plastic or wood putty, glue, nails, or screws. Select appropriate finishing ingredients such as paint, stain, lacquer, shellac, or varnish, depending on factors such as wood hardness and surface type. Prepare base for installation by removing unstable or unsuitable materials, compacting and grading the soil, draining or stabilizing weak or saturated soils and taking measures to prevent water penetration and migration of bedding sand.

Issue patterns to designated machine operators. Maintain pattern records for reference. Adjust controls to regulate temperature, pressure, feed, or flow of liquids or gases and times of prescribed reactions, according to knowledge of equipment and processes. Follow blueprints to produce specific designs. Design, create, and decorate entire pieces or specific parts of furniture, such as drawers for cabinets. Discuss the design with the client. Confer with customers to determine furniture colors or finishes.

Estimate materials required for production and manufacturing of products. Estimate costs for patternmaking jobs. Maintain production records, such as quantities, types, and dimensions of materials produced. Analyze work orders and specifications to confirm that production runs meet client needs and industry standards.

Trim, sand, scrape, or shave surfaces to prepare articles for finishing. Trim, smooth, and shape surfaces, and plane, shave, file, scrape, and sand models to attain specified shapes, using hand tools. Grind, sand, clean, or polish objects or parts to correct defects or to prepare surfaces for further finishing, using hand tools and power tools. Measure and mix chemical ingredients, according to specifications.

Remove old finishes and damaged or deteriorated parts, using hand tools, stripping tools, sandpaper, steel wool, abrasives, solvents, or dip baths. Remove accessories prior to finishing, and mask areas that should not be exposed to finishing processes or substances. Wash surfaces to prepare them for finish application.
Monitor material flow or instruments, such as temperature or pressure gauges, indicators, or meters, to ensure optimal processing conditions.
Clean and maintain equipment and tools, using hand tools, solvents, brushes, and rags.

Apply coatings, such as paint, ink, or lacquer, to protect or decorate workpiece surfaces, using spray guns, pens, or brushes. Clean surfaces of workpieces in preparation for coating and conceal blemishes in workpieces using fillers such as putty.

Examine and measure completed materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring devices such as tape measures, gauges, or calipers. Inspect machines or equipment for hazards, operating efficiency, malfunctions, wear, or leaks.
Read job orders and specifications to determine work procedures, production specifications, and materials required.
Weigh or measure materials, ingredients, or products to ensure conformance to requirements and collect samples for laboratory analysis.

Monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions and report or resolve problems. Observe production and equipment to ensure safe and efficient operation, and adjust machine components according to specifications.
Transfer materials, supplies, and products between work areas, using moving equipment or hand tools, and clean or sterilize production areas or equipment, using hoses, brushes, or chemical solutions.
Operate or tend machines to mix or blend materials, ensuring compliance with specifications, and stop machines when specified product qualities are obtained.
Record operational or production data in specified forms and maintain production logs to ensure traceability of processes.
Dump, pour, or load specified amounts of materials into machines or containers for processing or storage.
Replace worn or defective equipment parts using hand tools, and notify supervisors of needed repairs.
Test samples to determine conformance to specifications, using test equipment such as viscometers or pH meters. This includes testing for viscosity, acidity, and other physical properties.
Prepare materials by cutting, shaping, or joining them, and ensure dimensions match specified requirements.
Remove defective or substandard materials or products, and monitor and resolve any process-related issues.
Record gauge readings, materials used, processing times, or test results related to production processes.
Inspect and monitor chemical mixtures to maintain the desired concentrations and solutions.
Collect samples or signal workers to sample metal or materials for analysis.
Adjust, clean, and lubricate machines as necessary for optimal operation using appropriate tools.
Remove jams or blockages from machinery or processes, ensuring uninterrupted operation.
Unload and transport items to storage or processing areas.

Monitor batch, continuous flow, or hybrid biofuels production processes and stored biofuels products or secondary by-products until reused or transferred to users. Preprocess feedstock for physical, chemical, or biological fuel production processes. Perform routine maintenance on mechanical, electrical, or electronic equipment or instruments used in processing biofuels; clean and maintain the biofuels processing work area, ensuring compliance with safety regulations.

Weigh or measure materials, ingredients, or products at specified intervals to ensure conformance to requirements. Record data from operations, testing, and production on specified forms. Examine materials, ingredients, or products visually or with hands to ensure conformance to established standards. Collect biofuels samples and perform routine laboratory tests or analyses to assess biofuels quality.

Tend accessory equipment, such as pumps and conveyors, to move materials or ingredients through production processes. Start machines and turn handwheels or valves to engage feeding, cooling, and lubricating mechanisms. Move controls to start, stop, or adjust machinery and equipment that crushes, grinds, polishes, or blends materials.

Observe the operation of equipment to ensure continuity of flow, safety, and efficient operation, observing machine operation to detect workpiece defects or malfunctions. Reject defective products and readjust equipment to eliminate problems. Adjust controls to maintain temperatures and heating times, using thermal instruments and charts, and signal forklift operators to deposit or extract containers of parts.

Read work orders to determine production specifications and information, including operational sequences and furnace temperatures. Read blueprints or job orders to determine product specifications and tooling instructions and to plan operational sequences. Study sample parts, blueprints, drawings, or engineering information to determine methods or sequences of operations needed to fabricate products.

Inspect chains, belts, or scrolls for signs of wear; dislodge and clear jammed materials or other items from machinery and equipment, using hand tools; and examine workpieces for defects, measuring to determine accuracy of machine operations and ensuring proper lubrication or cooling. Inspect etched work for depth of etching, uniformity, and defects.

Remove parts from furnaces after specified times, air dry, or cool parts in water, oil brine, or other baths. Lay out, measure, and mark metal stock to display placement of cuts. Dispose of scrap or waste material in accordance with company policies and environmental regulations. Separate scrap waste and related materials for reuse, recycling, or disposal.

Calculate, measure, load, or mix refined feedstock used in biofuels production. Set mill gauges to specified fineness of grind. Load materials into machinery and equipment using hand tools. Move controls to light gas burners and adjust gas and water flow and flame temperature.

Instruct new workers in machine operation and supervise their training. Instruct other workers in machine setup and operation. Confer with engineering, supervisory, or manufacturing personnel to exchange technical information.

Repair, replace, and maintain equipment as needed using hand tools; make minor electrical and mechanical repairs and adjustments to machines. Diagnose machine tool malfunctions to determine the need for adjustments or repairs.

Install experimental parts or assemblies, such as hydraulic systems, electrical wiring, lubricants, or batteries into machines. Support metalworking projects from planning and fabrication through assembly, inspection, and testing, using knowledge of machine functions, metal properties, and mathematics. Fit and assemble parts to make or repair machine tools.

Set up and operate machines such as lathes, cutters, shears, borers, millers, grinders, presses, drills, or auxiliary machines, to make metallic and plastic workpieces. Set up and adjust machines, including basic or specialized machine tools used for precision machining operations.
Measure, examine, or test completed units to check for defects and ensure conformance to specifications, using precision instruments. Verify operational efficiency by operating equipment.

Engrave or stamp identifying symbols, letters, or numbers on products and clean, finish, and lubricate molds and mold parts. Trim or remove excess material using scrapers, knives, or band saws. Adjust depths and sizes of cuts by adjusting the heights of worktables or machine gauges.

Move controls or mount gears, cams, or templates in machines to set feed rates and cutting speeds, depths, and angles; monitor the feed and speed of machines during the machining process; and record operational data, such as pressure readings, lengths of strokes, feed rates, or speeds. Adjust machine stops or guides to specified lengths as indicated by scales, rules, or templates.
Repair mold defects, such as cracks or broken edges, using patterns, mold boxes, or hand tools.

Measure and cut products to specified dimensions, using measuring and cutting instruments; verify dimensions of products, using measuring instruments, such as calipers, vernier gauges, or protractors.
Smooth surfaces of molds and carvings using scraping tools, sandpaper, and rubbing stones, ensuring a smooth finish.
Measure ingredients and mix molding, casting materials, or sealing compounds to prescribed consistencies, according to formulas, and remove excess materials and level and smooth wet mold mixtures.

Construct or form molds for use in casting clay or plaster objects, using plaster, fiberglass, rubber, casting machines, patterns, or flasks; pour, pack, spread, or press plaster, concrete, or other materials into or around models or molds.
Position balls of clay in centers of potters' wheels, start motors or pump treadles, and adjust wheel speeds according to the feel of the clay while raising and shaping clay into wares.

Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications, using instruments such as micrometers, calipers, and magnifiers; examine finished ware for defects and measure dimensions using rules and thickness gauges.
Prepare work for sale or exhibition, maintaining relationships with retail, pottery, art, and resource networks to facilitate sale or exhibition of work.

Drill holes and cut or carve moldings and grooves in stone according to diagrams and patterns; carve designs or figures in full or bas relief on stone, employing knowledge of stone carving techniques to produce carvings consistent with designers' plans.
Operate gas or electric kilns to fire pottery pieces, mix and apply glazes using tools such as spray guns, and attach handles to pottery pieces.
Shape, bend, or join sections of glass using paddles and hand tools; blow tubing into specified shapes and place glass into dies or molds to form products.
Select chisels, pneumatic or surfacing tools, or sandblasting nozzles, and determine their sequence of use to shape, trim, or touch up roughed-out designs with appropriate tools to finish carvings.
Pack and ship pottery to stores or galleries for retail sale; design spaces to display pottery for sale; and operate drying chambers to dry or finish molded ceramic ware.
Maintain supplies of tools, equipment, and materials, and order additional supplies as needed; clean work area and remove excess materials from the workspace.
Record manufacturing information, such as quantities, sizes, or types of goods produced and verify accuracy of shapes and sizes of objects using calipers and templates.
Superimpose bent tubing on asbestos patterns to ensure accuracy and repair broken scrolls by replacing them with new sections of tubing.
Study artistic objects or graphic materials, such as models and blueprints, to plan carving or cutting techniques, and develop sketches of glass products into blueprint specifications.

Select machine tooling to be used, using knowledge of machine and production requirements; program and operate CNC machines; and set up, operate, or tend presses and forging machines to perform various operations on materials.
Lift and position workpieces manually or with hoists and secure them on machines using clamps; position and secure workpieces against fixtures or stops on machine beds or dies.

Verify conformance of machined work to specifications, and ensure that they meet quality standards by inspecting or measuring finished workpieces using measuring instruments such as micrometers and gauges.
Carve rough designs freehand or by chipping along marks on stone, using mallets and chisels, while moving fingers over surfaces of carvings to ensure a smooth finish.
Adjust pressures, temperatures, and trimming tool settings as required, while setting and adjusting machine controls according to product specifications.
Remove workpieces from machines, and check to ensure that they conform to specifications, using measuring instruments such as microscopes, gauges, and calipers.
Lift metal stock or workpieces using hoists, position them on machines using fasteners and hand tools, and select and install cutting tools and accessories according to specifications.
Study blueprints, work orders, or machining instructions to determine product specifications, tool requirements, and operational sequences.
Sharpen cutting tools and drill bits, using bench grinders; grind out burrs or sharp edges, and maintain stocks of machine parts and machining tools.

Turn valves to start the flow of coolant against cutting areas or to start airflow that blows cuttings away from kerfs; move machine controls to index workpieces and make necessary adjustments to correct problems.
Operate and maintain finishing machines that grind, drill, sand, bevel, decorate, wash, or polish glass or glass products, and adjust ram strokes of presses to specified lengths as needed.

Set up, operate, or tend machines to cut, bend, shape, or join metal or plastic materials; lift workpieces to machines manually or with hoists or cranes; and monitor machine operations to ensure compliance with specifications.

Install holding fixtures, attachments, and cutting tools to control stock and tool movement; verify specifications using gauges and measuring instruments; and observe machine operations to adjust for conformance.

Read work orders and production schedules to determine specifications; ensure materials, dimensions, and tolerances are correct; and measure completed workpieces for defects, maintaining quality control throughout the process.
Create functional or decorative objects by hand, using various techniques and materials; develop designs and concepts; and apply finishes to crafted items.
Pack products for shipping, confer with customers to assess needs and obtain feedback, and plan craft shows to market products and increase sales.
Monitor machine cycles and operations to detect jamming; troubleshoot and maintain machines; and perform minor repairs as necessary.
Clean work areas, machines, and tools to maintain safety and efficiency standards in manufacturing environments.
Perform minor assembly and adjustments on machinery and tools, including changing worn tools and modifying machine settings as needed, to reduce defects and enhance productivity.
Develop product packaging, display, and pricing strategies; and advertise crafted products using various media, including internet advertising.
Measure and verify dimensions, ensure proper lubrication, and monitor coolants to maintain optimal machine operation and product quality.
Establish zero reference points and layout machining locations using shop math; and input dimensions and control instructions into machine control panels.
Start machines and monitor operations while recording data; adjust the speed or actions of machines according to product specifications utilizing hand tools, gauges, and control panels.
Determine setup procedures and select machine dies and parts according to specifications; and install dies, machine screws, and sizing rings on relevant machines.
Calculate machine parameters such as speed and feed ratios; and write simple programs for computer-controlled machines if required.
Develop designs using specialized software and research craft trends to inform production and marketing strategies.
Conduct tests on physical properties of materials and maintain an inventory of supplies, ensuring availability for production needs.
Select materials based on their characteristics and intended use in craft production, ensuring quality and suitability.
Change dies, or adjust controls on machines that extrude thermoplastic or metal materials according to production line changes and specifications, ensuring proper operation.
Lift workpieces and adjust machine setups to accommodate specific project requirements; signal and coordinate with other workers when needed.
Interrupt machine operations as necessary for workpiece removal or tool changes, ensuring adherence to machining sequences while maintaining safety standards.
Perform additional tasks such as applying decorative patterns or marks, adjusting materials before production, or managing supplies to facilitate craft operations.
Examine components for defects in relation to specific crafting methods and ensure thorough preparation before the crafting process begins, focusing on quality control.

technology_skills.name: CPR Software FloorCOST Estimator for Excel

tools_used.name: Tungsten inert gas TIG welding equipment

detailed_work_activities.title: Record operational or production data.
Cut meat products.
Process animal carcasses.
Assemble tires.
Clean production equipment.
Measure dimensions of completed products or workpieces to verify conformance to specifications.
Mount attachments or tools onto production equipment.
Smooth surfaces with abrasive materials or tools.
Load materials into production equipment.
Prepare meat products for sale or consumption.
Cut industrial materials in preparation for fabrication or processing.
Install carpet or flooring.
Cut carpet, vinyl or other flexible materials.
Apply protective or decorative finishes to workpieces or products.
Finish concrete surfaces.
Install masonry materials.
Operate grinding equipment.
Mount materials or workpieces onto production equipment.
Operate metal or plastic forming equipment.
Operate cutting equipment.
Align parts or workpieces to ensure proper assembly.
Mark products, workpieces, or equipment with identifying information.
Apply adhesives to construction materials.
Trim excess material from workpieces.
Adjust equipment controls to regulate flow of production materials or products.
Read work orders or other instructions to determine product specifications or materials requirements.
Set equipment controls to meet cutting specifications.
Maintain production or processing equipment.
Adjust temperature controls of ovens or other heating equipment.
Clean workpieces or finished products.
Measure materials or objects for installation or assembly.
Remove products or workpieces from production equipment.
Review blueprints or other instructions to determine operational methods or sequences.
